Details: Granular activated carbon (GAC) is commonly used for the treatment of RDX-contaminated groundwater [1710].  Based on the results of one bench-scale study, RDX was very effectively removed by GAC at empty bed contact times (EBCT) of 20 minutes.  In RDX-spiked laboratory water, removal of RDX was still greater than 90 percent at more than 300,000 bed volumes and greater than 70 percent at more than 400,000 bed volumes.  However, breakthrough can occur much more quickly when other contaminants, such as perchlorate or HMX, are present [1712]. Another bench-scale study found that RDX was removed to levels below detection limits for groundwater contaminated with very low levels of RDX [1735].
